mnemonics: Sets: Union (U) is like a cup holding all items, Intersection (∩) is where two circles overlap, Complement (A') is everything outside set A.
keyPoints: A set is a well-defined collection of distinct objects.
Union combines all elements from both sets.
Intersection includes only elements common to both sets.
Complement of set A includes all elements not in A within the universal set.
Venn diagrams visually represent set operations.
commonMistakes: Confusing union with intersection: union is 'or', intersection is 'and'.
Forgetting that the complement is relative to the universal set.
Overlooking that sets contain distinct elements, so duplicates are not allowed.
